sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.6.7 plus terms — clinical observations and signs
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1–1.11.8 — reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.12.1–1.12.3 — follow-up chest X-rays
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

archive.title: Pneumonia: diagnosis and management
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
archive.setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
sources.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
sources.clinical_setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5–1.6 — community and acute risk criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.7 — emergency transfer, monitoring, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8 and 1.11 — source identification and investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.
